commands.review_summary:
The PR extends OpenAI-completions Anthropic cache-control detection to LiteLLM Claude-like model refs, adds transport-level payload tests, and updates LiteLLM/prompt-caching docs.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +76, Docs +13. Total +102 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main drops configured LiteLLM OpenAI-completions Claude cacheRetention before payload shaping, and this PR tests only the transport after options.cacheRetention is already supplied. I did not run a live LiteLLM-to-Anthropic proxy probe.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or human follow-up is needed to repair the explicit-retention gate and add real LiteLLM proof; automation should not claim the contributor's external setup proof for them.

target.title: fix(litellm): pass Anthropic cache control
target.body:
Closes #37966

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users routing Claude through LiteLLM would not get Anthropic prompt-cache markers even when they explicitly configured `cacheRetention`, causing repeated prompts to miss Anthropic cache creation/read behavior behind the proxy.

## Why This Change Was Made

OpenClaw already has Anthropic `cache_control` payload shaping for OpenAI-compatible Anthropic routes. This change extends that compatibility detection to LiteLLM Anthropic route names (`claude-*`, `anthropic/*`, and `anthropic.claude*`) while leaving non-Anthropic LiteLLM models and `cacheRetention: "none"` unmarked.

The provider docs and prompt-caching reference now call out the LiteLLM Anthropic exception instead of saying LiteLLM never receives prompt-cache hints.

## User Impact

Users can set `cacheRetention: "short"` or `cacheRetention: "long"` for LiteLLM-routed Claude models and have OpenClaw send Anthropic `cache_control` markers through the OpenAI-compatible payload. Non-Anthropic LiteLLM routes keep their existing payload shape.

## Evidence

- RED check: with the implementation temporarily removed, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` failed only the new LiteLLM Anthropic cache-control assertion because the system prompt stayed a plain string without `cache_control`.
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false docs:list`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts docs/providers/litellm.md docs/reference/prompt-caching.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
